qa.user_verbatim_complaint: Parallel-worker pipeline conflict resolution: (1) BASH_MAX_CONSECUTIVE=3 too low for parallel pipelines needing 4-5 consecutive Bash calls; (2) no named script for aggregate construction (inline Python forbidden); (3) no structured commit status contract; (4) no grant retry protocol; (5) inspector parallelism caused races on parallel-dev canonical.

spec_section_updates.section_4: Cycle verification 2026-05-24: All 8 ACs confirmed via grep, py_compile, and live execution. BASH_MAX_CONSECUTIVE=5 in gate.py:54. Step 0 in close.md at line 159, before Normal-path preflight at line 186. Step 6b in commit.md with full retry protocol. Structured Final Status Output section in changelog-analyst.md at line 563. aggregate-dev-report.py created (432 lines), py_compile exit 0, --dry-run exit 0 (action=skipped, 1 shard). No hardcoded absolute paths in any new code. Codex adversarial review (gpt-5.5 xhigh) found no blocking issues.

qa.complaint_resolution_evidence: All five root conflicts resolved: BASH_MAX_CONSECUTIVE raised to 5 (AC1); aggregate-dev-report.py named script created replacing forbidden inline Python (AC6, AC7); Structured Final Status Output section added to changelog-analyst.md (AC4); Step 6b retry protocol added to commit.md with --revoke-existing-for-task (AC5); Step 0 auto-aggregate and conditional sequential inspector dispatch added to close.md (AC2, AC3).
qa.overall_assessment: All 8 acceptance criteria verified and passing. Dev implemented all five coupled fixes (C, A+D, B_contract, P5) plus created the new aggregate-dev-report.py script. Codex adversarial review (gpt-5.5, xhigh reasoning) confirmed correctness with no blocking issues. No hardcoded absolute paths in new code. No inline Python/bash subprocesses. Regex patterns and NON_WORKER_LABELS in aggregate-dev-report.py are byte-identical to pretool-aggregate-check.py. py_compile passes for both new/modified Python files.

Dispatch conditional on `parallel_aggregate_written` / `parallel_workers` / shard count. Sequential dispatch path includes 'one at a time' / 'sequentially' language. Non-parallel path retains concurrent dispatch.
success_criteria_results.evidence: grep 'Step 1:' close.md → '### Step 1: Agent dispatch — three inspectors' (no unconditional 'in parallel'). Conditional logic for parallel_aggregate_written confirmed. Sequential dispatch language confirmed.

success_criteria_results.source_phrase: Structured Final Status Output section in changelog-analyst.md
success_criteria_results.criterion: '## Structured Final Status Output' section present; commit_status enum {committed, nothing_to_commit, nothing_to_commit_precommitted, failed}; auto_bulk_commits[]; failure_code table; THREE-STEP SHA-STABLE CHECK using git rev-parse, git show -s --format=%s, git show --name-only
success_criteria_results.details: Section exists at line 563. All 4 commit_status values present. auto_bulk_commits[] array defined with {repo_root, branch, sha} fields. failure_code table with retryable (grant_missing, grant_expired, grant_consumed) vs non-retryable (git_error, staging_error, hook_blocked, scope_violation). Three-step check: HEAD_SHA=$(git rev-parse --verify HEAD), git show -s --format=%s "$HEAD_SHA", git show --name-only --format= "$HEAD_SHA" | grep -v '^$'.
success_criteria_results.evidence: grep -n 'Structured Final Status Output' agents/changelog-analyst.md → line 563. All 4 status values confirmed by content inspection. Three git commands confirmed verbatim.

qa._success_criteria_rule: All 8 ACs from BA spec verified with evidence. Zero criteria skipped.

root_cause_verification.rationale: All five root conflicts directly addressed: (1) BASH_MAX_CONSECUTIVE raised to 5 — eliminates 3-call streak limit blocking parallel pipelines; (2) aggregate-dev-report.py named script exists — eliminates inline Python requirement; (3) Structured Final Status Output in changelog-analyst.md — gives commit.md retry protocol a parseable status contract; (4) Step 6b retry protocol with --revoke-existing-for-task — handles grant expiry/consumption without silent failure; (5) Step 0 auto-aggregate + conditional sequential inspector dispatch — prevents race conditions on shared canonical report.

build_verification.build_command: python3 -m py_compile scripts/aggregate-dev-report.py && python3 -m py_compile scripts/write-commit-grant.py
build_verification.build_output_summary: Both files compile without syntax errors. aggregate-dev-report.py --dry-run exits 0 with action=skipped.

regression_test_results.details: exit 0, action=skipped (1 shard detected, < 2 required for parallel aggregation — correct behavior)
PER_WORKER_ROLE_FIRST_RE, PER_WORKER_TASK_FIRST_RE, NON_WORKER_LABELS, NON_WORKER_LABEL_RE all byte-identical

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex (gpt-5.5, xhigh reasoning) reviewed all diffs and live test results across the full 8-AC scope. No blocking issues found that would change the PASS verdict. Dev had already addressed all 4 BLOCKERs and 6/9 MAJOR items identified in the dev-cycle codex consultation. QA codex review confirmed: canonical_path uses full task_id (not bare timestamp); shard scanning isolation correct; canonical validation loads and compares existing; SID scoped correctly in _revoke_grants_for_task; ${CONTROL_ROOT}/scripts/ used (not hardcoded). Two deferred items (DRYRUN not in commit_status enum; no unambiguous final-line sentinel in changelog-analyst.md) are out-of-scope per BA spec.
codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: No changes to verdict. All blocking findings were already resolved by dev. Deferred items (codex findings 8 and 9) confirmed out-of-scope per BA spec and do not affect this cycle's verdict.
